game_name: Mighty Morphin Power Rangers: The Movie

design_philosophy.core_loop: Progress through linear stages as civilian, fill morphing meter with power-ups, transform into powered Ranger, defeat bosses with signature weapons and special attacks
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management of morphing meter; balancing civilian vulnerability against morphed power state; progression gating on transformation availability
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Transform into a Power Ranger and defeat enemies using signature weapons; cooperative two-player ranger team action
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Mandatory civilian-to-morphed transformation mechanic creates distinct gameplay phases; morphing meter as primary progression gate; Final Fight-style beat-em-up formula applied to licensed IP with multi-character roster

difficulty.lives_system: Lives counter present; can be increased via debug features to maximum of 8 before wrapping to 0
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based checkpointing; no mid-level checkpoints documented
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Six playable characters with differentiated stats (Billy/Kimberly weaker but mobile); difficulty appears accessible per GamePro review praise of 'accessible difficulty and controls'

progression.upgrade_system: Morphing meter fills via thunderbolt power-ups dropped by enemies; morphed state provides powered kick, signature weapon access, and special attacks; meter resets per transformation
progression.unlock_mechanism: Seven sequential levels with mandatory boss defeats; final level (Ivan Ooze) accessible only after level 6 completion
replayability_hooks: Six playable characters with distinct stats and signature weapons
Multi-plane stage layout encourages exploration of vertical space
Hidden level select accessible via PAR codes suggests developer intended alternative play paths

world_building_techniques: Animated morphing sequences using digitized video from Season 2 transformation
Level progression follows film narrative (Ivan Ooze as antagonist)
Familiar TV monsters integrated as mid-level bosses connecting to show canon
Ninja Megazord appearance in ending sequence ties to film climax

boss_design.philosophy: Escalation from TV-show monsters to original creations to film's primary antagonist; bosses test signature weapon special attacks and morphed state management
notable_bosses: Five TV show Season 2 monsters (first five levels)

faction_indicators: Ranger color assignments to six playable characters
Lord Zedd's Putty Patrol as unified enemy faction
Civilian vs. Morphed state as in-world social markers of power/authority
danger_signals: Depleted morphing meter forces civilian state vulnerability
Boss encounters trigger mandatory morphing sequences revealing moment of transformation danger
Ivan Ooze as film's existential threat requiring final morphed confrontation
environmental_storytelling: Multi-plane stage layouts suggest expansive battle environments
Thunderbolt power-up distribution by defeated enemies reinforces combat-as-resource-generation loop
Ending Ninja Megazord appearance grounds ranger progression in mecha-suit technology
